ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists specifically for Pepero or Korean coated biscuit sticks; closest defensible match is the generic cookie/baked good class. Pepero is a coated biscuit stick product.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A03SJ (Biscuits and cookies) is the closest defensible category for Pepero coated biscuit sticks. No specific Pepero or Korean coated stick sub-code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Pepero or Korean coated biscuit sticks; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of Pepero is approximately 40 g (roughly one small box or 8–10 sticks), providing about 206 kcal, 12.4 g sugar, and 10.7 g fat.

nutrient_source.title: Pepero (any flavour) — Singapore HPB Foodo nutrient database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Foodo database entry for Pepero (any flavour), cr_id F11080044. All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated at approximately 13.5 g/100g based on typical Pepero formulation (palm oil coating); this specific sub-fraction is not separately reported in the HPB entry and is documented as an estimate cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central entry for similar chocolate-coated biscuit sticks (USDA SR Legacy item 'Crackers, cookie-type, chocolate-coated'). Detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data are not reported in the HPB Foodo entry for this record nor in USDA FoodData Central for this specific product; those fields are set to -1 as documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Pepero in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 62 is estimated using the closest analogous food: chocolate-coated biscuit/cookie sticks with refined wheat flour base, which fall in the medium GI range (55–70) per Atkinson et al. 2008 and Brand-Miller GI tables. Glycemic load per 40 g serving calculated as: (62 × 24.3 g available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = 15.1, rounded to 15.0.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (31.4 g/100g) and saturated fat (est. 13.5 g/100g) from palm oil coating
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid regular consumption of Pepero; the high sugar load (31.4 g/100g) promotes insulin-mediated sodium retention, and the palm oil-derived saturated fat contributes to arterial stiffness and endothelial dysfunction, both of which elevate blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar 31.4 g/100g and refined carbohydrate 60.7 g/100g with medium GI (est. 62) causing rapid postprandial glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: Pepero is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; sugar at 31.4 g/100g far exceeds the 20 g/100g glycaemic ceiling, and the refined wheat flour base (60.7 g/100g carbohydrate) with medium GI of approximately 62 produces significant postprandial blood glucose spikes even in small portions — avoid entirely for blood glucose management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat est. 13.5 g/100g from palm oil, trans fat est. 0.2 g/100g, and high sugar 31.4 g/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Pepero poses a high cardiovascular risk due to its combination of palm oil-derived saturated fat (est. 13.5 g/100g), trace trans fat (est. 0.2 g/100g from processing), and high sugar (31.4 g/100g), all of which raise LDL cholesterol, promote systemic inflammation, and increase atherogenic risk; individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should avoid this product.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat from palm oil and trace trans fat are pro-inflammatory; refined sugar promotes inflammatory mediators
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Pepero; palm oil-derived saturated fat (est. 13.5 g/100g) promotes TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory cytokines, trace trans fat (est. 0.2 g/100g) is strongly pro-inflammatory at any amount, and refined sugar (31.4 g/100g) further amplifies systemic inflammatory mediators.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pepero (빼빼로) is a popular South Korean ultra-processed snack manufactured by Lotte Confectionery, consisting of thin, crispy baked wheat-flour biscuit sticks dipped or coated in a flavoured coating — most commonly chocolate, but also available in almond, strawberry, white chocolate, and other variants. The biscuit stick base is made from refined wheat flour, sugar, vegetable oil, and leavening agents, producing a light, crunchy texture. The coating adds sweetness, richness, and flavour complexity. Per 100 g, Pepero delivers 516 kcal, 9.5 g protein, 26.7 g fat (predominantly from palm oil in the coating), and 60.7 g carbohydrate including 31.4 g sugar and 3.8 g dietary fibre. The high sugar content (31.4 g/100g) and refined carbohydrate base make this food clinically unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The palm oil-derived saturated fat (est. 13.5 g/100g) and trace trans fat (est. 0.2 g/100g) further elevate cardiovascular and inflammatory risk. Pepero is widely consumed as a snack in South Korea and across Southeast Asia, and is culturally associated with Pepero Day (November 11), a South Korean confectionery gifting occasion. It is commonly found in Korean convenience stores, supermarkets, and Asian grocery outlets globally. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 62 (medium GI) based on analogous chocolate-coated biscuit sticks, yielding a glycemic load of approximately 15 per standard 40 g serving.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Foodo database (cr_id F11080044) at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and trans fat are estimated from typical Pepero formulation cross-referenced against USDA SR Legacy analogous entry for chocolate-coated biscuit sticks, documented as estimates. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008 for chocolate-coated biscuit sticks. Overall confidence is high given the primary HPB source for core macros.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (13.5 g/100g) and trans fat (0.2 g/100g) are estimates derived from typical Pepero product formulation (palm oil coating) cross-referenced against USDA SR Legacy entry for chocolate-coated biscuit sticks; these values are not separately reported in the HPB Foodo entry for this record. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for Pepero (any flavour) are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry (F11080044) nor in USDA FoodData Central for this specific product as of 2026; no approved source consulted provides these sub-fractions for this food item. 3. calcium_mg is reported as 0.0 and iron_mg is reported as 0.0 per HPB Foodo source (F11080044); these are confirmed zero values from the approved source. 4. GI of 62 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Pepero exists in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2026. 5. Halal status is not confirmed for all Pepero variants; some variants may contain non-halal ingredients — consumers should verify product label. 6. Input dietary_tags included 'wholegrain' and 'high_fibre' which are not supported by the nutrient profile (refined wheat flour base, fibre 3.78 g/100g does not meet high-fibre threshold); these tags have been removed from the output.
